title: gagea pratensis subsp. nova
intro: Gagea pratensis subsp. nova, also known as the lesser celandine or green celandine, is a perennial flowering plant that belongs to the lily family and is native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds that can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Gagea pratensis subsp. nova include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, as well as its potential to treat digestive issues and respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and tinctures, are often used to harness its medicinal properties and can be found in some traditional herbal remedies.
